name: Georgia

colors.symbolism: innocence, purity, chastity, perfection and wisdom; also peace

description: A white field bearing a large red Saint George's cross extending to all four edges; in each of the four white quarters thus formed, a smaller red cross bottony (Bolnisi-style cross with flared arms).
history: Known as the 'Five Cross Flag' (ხუთჯვრიანი დროშა). The design has medieval origins and has been associated with the Kingdom of Georgia from the 13th–14th centuries; it appears on European maps and in heraldic compendia from the 14th century onward. After Georgian independence from the Soviet Union in 1991 the country initially used the 1918–1921 wine-red flag with a black-and-white canton, but the five-cross flag was readopted as the national flag on 14 January 2004 following the Rose Revolution, having previously been the flag of the United National Movement.

features.symbolism: the Cross of Saint George; Christianity and the long Christian heritage of Georgia

features.role: four small Bolnisi crosses (cross bottony / cross pattée) in the four quarters
features.symbolism: the four small crosses, together with the central Saint George's cross, form the 'Five-Cross Flag' (ბექბექაი) traditionally associated with the medieval Kingdom of Georgia under King George V the Brilliant; the five crosses are sometimes interpreted as Christ and the four evangelists
